prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a nearly black screen. A “Classic Commercial Archives” circular watermark appears at lower left, and a faint “@ClassicCommercialArchives” watermark runs across the lower center/right.

It then cuts to an outdoor street-sign setting. A yellow rectangular sign mounted on a post reads “PLEASANT STREET” in large black lettering. The background shows greenery and a residential-looking outdoor area with trees and shade.

A fast blurred transition follows, with the sign or scene streaking horizontally, suggesting a quick camera move or graphic wipe.

The image resolves into a product shot: a box labeled “DRISTAN” with “DECONGESTANT TABLETS” beneath it. The box is shown against a pale background, with overlaid or transitioning imagery of the earlier street sign still faintly visible. Text on the box reads “COLDS & HAY FEVER” and “SINUS CONGESTION.”

Next, the product packaging remains faintly in the background while individual medicine items are displayed in front: a brown/orange capsule-like container or capsule form, a cluster of small round tablets or pellets, and a blue tablet/caplet-like object. The background is mostly white or light gray, with the Dristan box ghosted behind the objects.

Later, the scene shifts back outdoors to a sunny yard or garden. A woman is visible standing beside or near a dark-colored dog. She appears to be bending or reaching toward the dog while holding or touching something near it. Trees, shrubs, grass, and part of a house or porch are visible in the background. Overlaid text at the bottom reads “USE ONLY AS DIRECTED.” There is also faint product-related overlay text partially visible.

The commercial then returns to the street-sign motif. A similar yellow sign now reads “HAYFEVER STREET,” again mounted on a post in the same type of outdoor setting.

The final frame shows a sign-like product display mounted on the post. It features the Dristan packaging design: “DRISTAN,” “DECONGESTANT TABLETS,” and the conditions “COLDS & HAY FEVER” and “SINUS CONGESTION.” At the bottom of the frame, large white text reads: “EXTRA RELIEF MEDICINE THE OTHERS DON’T HAVE.” The outdoor residential background remains visible behind the sign.
